summary: Australian politics dominated today's news as the Coalition fended off One Nation challenges in recent elections, with Victorian Liberals continuing to resist committing to any formal deals with the party ahead of November's state poll. A fatal systems failure emerged in a coroner's report finding that an emergency services breakdown contributed to a preventable death, while a man was arrested in Sydney following a triple homicide that police allege involved his parents and brother. Sports coverage remained extensive with various AFL and NRL matches generating headlines, while international stories ranged from a stranded humpback whale's rescue to geopolitical concerns about Middle Eastern shipping routes affecting Australian interests. Health and science stories included research on wastewater drug monitoring in Western Australia and potential Alzheimer's prevention breakthroughs.
